content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

January 21, 1983


Honorable William M. Bennett
Chair
Board of Equalization
1020 N Street
Sacramento, CA 9814

Re: Our File No. A-83-007

Dear Mr. Bennett:

Thank you for your letter of December 9, 1982, regarding the
applicability of Government Code Section 84308 to members of the
Board of Equalization. Since you posed your question on behalf
of all the Board members, this letter constitutes advice
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114 as to each of the
Board members.

You asked whether, as constitutional officers, the members
of the Board of Equalization are covered by Section 84308. Our
answer is that the Board is not covered by Section 84308.

Section 84308(d) excludes certain types of boards and
commissions from the coverage of Section 84308. Section
84308(d) provides that:

State constitutional officers who serve on a board or
commission as a requirement of their constitutional
office are not subject to the provisions of this
section.

Since members of the Board of Equalization serve on the Board as
a requirement of their constitutional office, they are exempt
from the provisions of Section 84308 when they act in their
capacity as members of the Board.

Section 84308(d) contains another exemption which is
applicable to the Board:

Honorable William M. Bennett
January 21, 1983
Page 2


Legislative bodies such as city councils, county boards
of supervisors, and the State Legislature while acting
as a board or committee thereof are exempted from the
coverage of this section.

The Commission has interpreted this provision in a newly
approved regulation,* 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1(a):

(a)  A "legislative body" is excluded by
Government Code Section 84308(d) from the coverage of
Government Code Sections 84308(a), (b), and (c) if its
entire membership consists of elected officers directly
elected by the voters to serve on that body. As used
herein, the term "voters" has the same meaning as in
Elections Code Section 18.

Since the members of the Board of Equalization are directly
elected, the Board is a legislative body which is exempt from
Section 84308.

I hope this answers your question. If you have any further
questions with respect to this law, please feel free to get in
touch with me.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure

*This regulation has been approved by the Commission, but
has not yet been filed with the Secretary of State and thus is
not yet effective. In the meantime, it reflects the advice
being given by the Commission and the staff under Government
Code Section 83114.

CITY OF ANAHEIM, CALIFORNIA
CIVIC CENTER, 200 South Anaheim Boulevard, Third Floor
Anaheim, California 92805

Office of
CITY ATTORNEY

January 14, 1983

Robert E. Leidigh, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Interpretation of FPPC
Administrative Regulation
18438.1 - Re:
Levine Bill
Government Code 84308

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

With reference to our telephone conversation of January
14, 1983, I would appreciate an informal letter from your
office concerning the FPPC interpretation of the Levine Bill
(Government Code 84308) with respect to the exemption of a
City Council from the provisions of the law while acting as a
body or committee thereof.

Specifically, our question relates to the exemption of
a City Council while acting as a Redevelopment Agency and or
Housing Authority when all members of the City Council also
act as the Redevelopment Agency and Housing Authority.

The letter requested would confirm your interpretation
of Administrative Regulation 18438.1 (a) and (b) exempting
the City Council members under the above circumstances.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

WILLIAM P. HOPKINS
CITY ATTORNEY

WPH/vh

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660            322-5901          322-6441                322-6444


January 21, 1983


Honorable William M. Bennett
Chair
Board of Equalization
1020 N Street
Sacramento, CA 98l4

Re: Our File No. A-83-007

Dear Mr. Bennett:

     Thank you for your letter of December 9, 1982, regarding the
applicability of Government Code Section 84308 to members of the
Board of Equalization.  Since you posed your question on behalf
of all the Board members, this letter constitutes advice
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114 as to each of the
Board members.

     You asked whether, as constitutional officers, the members
of the Board of Equalization are covered by Section 84308.  Our
answer is that the Board is not covered by Section 84308.

     Section 84308(d) excludes certain types of boards and
commissions from the coverage of Section 84308.  Section
84308(d) provides that:

          State constitutional officers who serve on a board or
          commission as a requirement of their constitutional
          office are not subject to the provisions of this
          section.

     Since members of the Board of Equalization serve on the Board as
a requirement of their constitutional office, they are exempt
from the provisions of Section 84308 when they act in their
capacity as members of the Board.

     Section 84308(d) contains another exemption which is
applicable to the Board:

Honorable William M. Bennett
January 21, 1983
Page 2


Legislative bodies such as city councils, county boards
of supervisors, and the State Legislature while acting
as a board or committee thereof are exempted from the
coverage of this section.

The Commission has interpreted this provision in a newly
approved regulation,* 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1(a):

(a) A "legislative body" is excluded by
Government Code Section 84308(d) from the coverage of
Government Code Sections 84308(a), (b), and (c) if its
entire membership consists of elected officers directly
elected by the voters to serve on that body. As used
herein, the term "voters" has the same meaning as in
Elections Code Section 18.

Since the members of the Board of Equalization are directly
elected, the Board is a legislative body which is exempt from
Section 84308.

I hope this answers your question. If you have any further
questions with respect to this law, please feel free to get in
touch with me.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure

*This regulation has been approved by the Commission, but
has not yet been filed with the Secretary of State and thus is
not yet effective. In the meantime, it reflects the advice
being given by the Commission and the staff under Government
Code Section 83114.

WILLIAM M BENNETT
MEMBER STATE SENATE 27TH DISTRICT

December 9, 1982


Ms. Barbara Milman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 X Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Milman:

It has just been brought to my attention that your
Board will soon be considering the adoption of regulations
to implement Chapter 1049 of the Statutes of 1982 (AB 1049)
relating to the acceptance of campaign contributions by
elected and appointed officers who serve on quasi-judicial
boards commissions that issue licenses, permits or other
entitlements for use.

While the contemplated regulations do help to clarify
the new Section 34303 of the Government Code, they are
deficient in one respect. The deficiency to which I refer
is the omission of any reference to the specific exclusion
of state constitutional officers from the limitations of
Section 34303 contained in subsection (d) thereof. This
exclusion should be explicitly set out in the regulation.

It is my understanding that you have discussed this
matter with Jim Delaney, the Board's chief Counsel, and
that you are disposed toward recommending our suggested
change. Please call on Mr. Delaney for any assistance he
can render and advise him of the date and the time of the
hearing at which your Board will consider this matter.

Thank you in advance for making our concerns known to
your Board.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Chairman

WMB:ss

cc: Honorable Richard Vining          Mr. Douglas D. Bell
    Honorable Conway B. Collis         Mr. P. J. Delaney
    Honorable Ernest J. Dronenburg, Jr.
    Honorable Kenneth Cory

[illegible text at bottom]

Betsy Strauss
January 21, 1983
Page 3


commissions (such as the Bay Area Air Quality Management
District) and joint powers agencies. However, the exemption
does apply to a city council sitting as the city redevelopment
agency or the city housing authority where the makeup of such a
board consists entirely of members of the city council.

I trust that this letter accurately confirms our discussion
and adequately expresses the general rule to be followed in
applying the exemption. Should you have any further questions,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                       Sincerely,

                                       [signature]
                                       Robert E. Leidigh
                                       Counsel
                                       Legal Division

REL:plh
cc:  William P. Hopkins
     Anaheim City Attorney
